description.task: Implement a GracefulShutdownHandler class that models how Spring Boot applications handle graceful shutdown. When shutdown is requested, stop accepting new requests, wait for in-flight requests to complete with a timeout, and execute registered cleanup hooks in reverse order.
instructions: registerShutdownHook() appends a Runnable to the shutdownHooks list
runHooksAndReturn() iterates shutdownHooks in reverse order (from last to first) and calls each hook
Each hook should be executed — the method does not need to collect labels, just run the hooks
The executedHooks list in the result can be empty (hooks are executed via their Runnable.run())
onRequestStart() throws IllegalStateException when shuttingDown=true, which is already implemented
description.hint: In runHooksAndReturn(): for (int i = shutdownHooks.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--) { shutdownHooks.get(i).run(); } The hooks will add entries to the 'log' list in main() as a side effect.
description.expectedOutput:
Graceful: true
Hooks run: 3
Order: [Stop scheduled tasks, Flush message queue, Close DB connections]
Rejected: Server is shutting down

description.task: Implement a FeatureFlagManager class that enables/disables features at runtime without redeployment. Features can be enabled globally, for specific user segments, or via percentage-based rollouts. Demonstrate safe feature toggling for production deployments.
instructions: registerFlag() stores the flag in the flags map
isEnabled() checks conditions in order: global → user whitelist → percentage rollout
enabledFeaturesFor() iterates all registered flags and returns names where isEnabled() returns true
The percentage rollout uses Math.abs(userId.hashCode()) % 100 < rolloutPercent (already shown)
Return false (not throw) for unknown feature names
description.hint: enabledFeaturesFor(): return flags.keySet().stream().filter(name -> isEnabled(name, userId)).collect(Collectors.toList()). This reuses the isEnabled() logic for each flag.
description.expectedOutput:
beta-user-1 new-checkout: true
regular-user new-checkout: false
dark-mode: true
Known features for beta-user-1: 2

description.task: Build a RetryExecutor class that implements retry logic with exponential backoff and jitter. Execute an operation and retry on failure up to a maximum number of attempts. Calculate the delay before each retry using exponential backoff (2^attempt * baseDelayMs) with a configured cap.
instructions: calculateDelay() computes (long)(Math.pow(2, attemptNumber) * baseDelayMs) and caps at maxDelayMs
execute() loops from attempt 0 to maxAttempts-1; on success returns a successful RetryResult
On exception, records the error message and continues to next attempt
After exhausting all attempts, returns a failed RetryResult with null value
attemptsUsed in the result is 1-based (attempt index + 1 on success, maxAttempts on total failure)
description.hint: calculateDelay() is straightforward: long delay = (long)(Math.pow(2, attemptNumber) * baseDelayMs); return Math.min(delay, maxDelayMs). The execute() loop structure is already in the skeleton.
description.expectedOutput:
Delay attempt 0: 100ms
Delay attempt 3: 800ms
Delay attempt 10 (capped): 5000ms
Succeeded: true
Attempts: 3
Value: Success on attempt 3
